text: Enantioselective allylation is a cornerstone of organic synthesis, frequently employed to generate chiral homoallylic alcohols and amines. These compounds are essential building blocks for constructing a diverse array of natural products and therapeutic agents. Over the past decade, the field has seen a surge in research focused specifically on the catalytic asymmetric allylation of carbonyl compounds and imines using allylic boronates. This review explores recent advancements in the area, highlighting the evolution from stoichiometric reagents to highly efficient catalytic systems, and discusses the development of metal-mediated, acid-catalyzed, and organocatalytic methods.
